title: CircuitBreaker Not Wired
description: AsyncCircuitBreaker exists and is built via build_circuit_breaker but is not used or injected into any channel_pool or router — additionally, record_success()/record_failure() are private methods (not public) making external integration impossible
impact: No protection from cascade failures — a single channel failure can overwhelm the system; external integration impossible due to lack of public record methods
proposed_solution: Add public record_success and record_failure methods to AsyncCircuitBreaker; wire CircuitBreaker around AsyncChannelPool.acquire(); call .record_success()/.record_failure() after each send in the upper layer; modify build_channel_pool to accept optional circuit_breaker
evidence: network/resilience/circuit_breaker.py:45-58; wiring/factories/network_provider.py build_circuit_breaker unused; circuit_breaker._record_success() and _record_failure() are private and cannot be called externally

title: DNSResolver Not Used by Assembler
description: StandardDnsResolver is built via factory but CommunicationFabricAssembler does not use it — endpoint resolution is disconnected
impact: service discovery ineffective — endpoints are hardcoded in config and not resolved dynamically
proposed_solution: Inject dns_resolver into SessionRouterAdapter so any missing channel gets DNS-resolved before failure
evidence: network/adapters/dns_resolver.py exists; wiring/factories/network_provider.py exports DNSResolver but not used

title: ConfigResolver Not Used by Assembler
description: ConfigServiceResolver is built but not wired — services defined in YAML do not actually affect routing
impact: YAML service configuration does not actually affect routing — discrepancy between docs and reality
proposed_solution: Inject config_resolver into SessionRouterAdapter as fallback source for endpoints
evidence: network/adapters/config_resolver.py exists; wiring/factories/network_provider.py exports ConfigResolver but unused

title: LoadBalancer Strategies Untested
description: RoundRobinStrategy and LeastActiveStrategy have select() functionality but no tests with real channel pool — plus RoundRobinStrategy has lock (asyncio.Lock()) that is never used
impact: Cannot guarantee correct load distribution under concurrency; unused lock causes maintenance confusion
proposed_solution: Write tests: (1) RoundRobin distributes evenly across 3 channels (2) LeastActive selects least loaded (3) unhealthy channel filtering (4) remove unused lock or use it properly in select()
evidence: network/algorithms/round_robin.py:16-28; asyncio.Lock created in __init__ and never used in select()

title: No Weighted or Adaptive Routing
description: Only two algorithms available (round_robin, least_active) — no Weighted Round Robin, no Latency-Aware, no Random
impact: In production you may need channels with different capacities (c1=100rps, c2=10rps) — equal distribution not suitable
proposed_solution: Add WeightedRoundRobinStrategy and RandomStrategy to algorithms/; expand ILoadBalancerStrategy to support weights

title: No Channel Health Probing
description: AsyncChannelPool does not periodically probe channel health — router may select a dead channel; current health_check() only cleans dead channels without sending pings
impact: Sending messages to dead channels causes TransportError even with circuit breaker
proposed_solution: Add PeriodicHealthChecker to monitor channels via send/receive health ping at each interval; update is_healthy in ChannelRef; current health_check() does not check liveness but only cleans dead channels

title: No Connection Pool Retry / Timeout
description: AsyncChannelPool.acquire() calls TransportFactory.create_channel() without asyncio.wait_for() — transient failures or connection hangs cause acquire() to hang forever; no connect_retry
impact: acquire() hanging forever prevents whole pool from working; transient connection failures cause request failure
proposed_solution: Add connect_timeout (via asyncio.wait_for()) and connect_retry_count to AsyncChannelPool.__init__() and acquire(); severity should be high because pool can hang forever without timeout

title: RoundRobinStrategy Lock Never Acquired
description: asyncio.Lock is created in __init__ line 16 but never used in select() lines 18-31 — self._index += 1 is not protected
impact: self._index += 1 is not thread-safe under concurrency (race conditions); misleading code implies protection that doesn't exist
proposed_solution: Either use lock properly in select() or remove lock entirely to simplify code
evidence: network/algorithms/round_robin.py:16; self._lock created but never used

title: SessionRouterAdapter Hardcodes ChannelState String Values
description: resolve() line 38 compares channel.state.value in (\active\" \"connecting\") instead of using ChannelState.ACTIVE and ChannelState.CONNECTING"
impact: If ChannelState enum values change, routing breaks silently without compile-time error
proposed_solution: Replace string comparison with channel.state in (ChannelState.ACTIVE, ChannelState.CONNECTING)

title: network/protocol.py Duplicate / Dead Imports
description: network/protocol.py contains duplicate imports (lines 15-17) and a stale comment \# network/protocol.py (added section only)\""
impact: Pollutes namespace with unused imports; confuses code readers
proposed_solution: Remove lines 15-17 (duplicate imports and stale comment)

title: ConfigServiceResolver resolve() Port Filter is No-op
description: resolve() line 52: [e if e.port != port else ...] returns all endpoints regardless of requested port — no actual filtering occurs
impact: Calling resolve(name, port=8080) returns endpoints on port 80 without filtering — unexpected behavior
proposed_solution: Either fix the logic to filter endpoints by port or remove port parameter entirely

title: AsyncCircuitBreaker No Public record_success/record_failure
description: _record_success() and _record_failure() are private methods — cannot be called from outside the class (like middleware)
impact: Cannot implement NET-001 proposed solution because required methods are private; prevents external circuit breaker integration
proposed_solution: Add public async record_success() and async record_failure() methods that call the private versions
evidence: network/resilience/circuit_breaker.py:68-88; methods created as async def _record_success and _record_failure (private)

title: network/__init__.py __all__ misses SessionRouterAdapter
description: SessionRouterAdapter is imported on line 21 but not included in __all__ (lines 36-59); from network import * will not export it
impact: Inconsistency between import and export; from network import * won't include SessionRouterAdapter
